no_ssd_reason: No single SSD for unspeciated As; use As(V) SSD (conservative default, oxygenated conditions) or As(III) SSD (reducing conditions)
dgv_conditions: µg As/L total dissolved; DGVs shown are As(V) values (conservative default). Speciate where possible.
notes: Unspeciated As: defaults to As(V) SSD. This is the conservative max-PAF envelope of the two speciation SSDs (verified: As(V) gives a higher PAF than As(III) at all realistic concentrations 0.1-5000 ug/L; they cross only above ~10 mg/L). Although leachate is reducing (As(III) dominant) and As(III) is more toxic to the single most-sensitive diatom, the whole-assemblage As(V) SSD sits lower, so defaulting to As(V) is conservative regardless of speciation. Supply As(III)/As(V) explicitly to use a specific SSD. DGVs listed are the As(V) values.

ANZECC adopted MR TV (acute SSD ÷ ACR=25.10) over HR TV (chronic HC5=0.9 µg/L) as HR did not protect against acute toxicity. Both chronic and acute datasets in observations CSV. observations_analyte='g-BHC_acute' for ANZECC-matching fit. NOTE: Warne Table 4 lists dgv_99pct=0.0671 µg/L (=HC1÷10, i.e. default ACR) rather than HC1÷25.10=0.0267 µg/L; this is a Warne arithmetic error — all other HC levels (HC5, HC10, HC20) correctly divide by ACR=25.10. Our computed HC1/25.10=0.0267 is arithmetically correct; the dgv_99pct reference value of 0.07 reflects the Warne publication error.
